pages.title: Four Reliability Gates
pages.kicker: A recording becomes useful context only when every contract holds

cards.title: Capture integrity
cards.body: Make recording state visible, preserve timestamps, and recover local audio after interruption.

cards.title: Transfer integrity
cards.body: Resume unstable links without missing, reordering, or duplicating audio chunks.

cards.title: Semantic integrity
cards.body: Trace every generated note or task back to transcript spans and audio time ranges.

cards.title: Policy integrity
cards.body: Carry consent, allowed uses, retention, deletion, and audit state through every derivative.

pages.title: Latency Is A Readiness Ladder
pages.kicker: Offline voice systems should expose when audio, text, context, and actions become safe to use.

cards.title: Audio durable
cards.body: The complete source survives device, app, and network interruption.

cards.title: Transcript ready
cards.body: Words and timing reach a declared version with confidence.

cards.title: Context ready
cards.body: Speakers, provenance, corrections, and permissions are attached.

cards.title: Action ready
cards.body: Policy decides whether to draft, confirm, commit, or abstain.

pages.title: Consent Must Travel With Data
pages.kicker: Recording permission is not a one-time checkbox; it constrains every downstream artifact and action.

cards.title: Visible capture
cards.body: People can understand when recording starts, stops, and uploads.

cards.title: Permission scope
cards.body: Purpose, participants, tenant, and allowed processing remain attached.

cards.title: Retention
cards.body: Deletion and expiry apply to source audio and derived artifacts.

cards.title: Tool authority
cards.body: Context access does not automatically grant permission to act.
